title: T-008 — maintenance_kpis MV + pg_cron daily refresh (MNT §9.16)
prompt:
# T-008 — maintenance_kpis materialized view

PRD: docs/prd/13-MAINTENANCE-PRD.md §9.16, §10.1 (MNT-006 Manager overview), §3.3 (KPIs)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `maintenance_kpis` materialized view aggregating monthly per-equipment counters (preventive_count, reactive_count, pm_on_time_count, auto_downtime_count, avg_mttr_min, total_cost). Add daily pg_cron refresh job at 02:30 UTC.

## Pre-flight
Verify `maintenance_work_orders` (T-004) exists. Verify `pg_cron` extension enabled by Foundation/operations migrations — if absent, fall back to manual REFRESH command + a comment that 14-multi-site/operations will enable pg_cron.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration `packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_kpis_mv.sql` with:
   - CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW `maintenance_kpis` per PRD §9.16 (corrected pm_on_time logic — the PRD has a placeholder `<scheduled_date>`; replace with `(SELECT next_due_date FROM maintenance_schedules WHERE id = mwo.schedule_id)` joined subquery).
   - CREATE UNIQUE INDEX on (org_id, site_id, equipment_id, month) so REFRESH CONCURRENTLY works.
   - SECURITY INVOKER (or SECURITY DEFINER with explicit `app.current_org_id()` check guard — pick INVOKER so RLS is enforced for downstream consumers).
2. pg_cron schedule: `SELECT cron.schedule('refresh_maintenance_kpis','30 2 * * *', $$REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY maintenance_kpis$$);`. Wrap in `IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='pg_cron')` so migration is portable.
3. Vitest: insert ~10 MWOs across two orgs/months; manually REFRESH; assert per-month per-org rows match expected counts; assert UNIQUE index supports CONCURRENTLY refresh; assert cron job entry present when pg_cron extension is loaded.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/NNNN_maintenance_kpis_mv.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/maintenance-kpis-mv.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration runs, when `maintenance_kpis` is queried, then it returns rows grouped by org_id+site_id+equipment_id+month with correct counters per §9.16.
2. Given 6 MWOs in orgA (3 preventive completed, 2 reactive completed, 1 cancelled), when MV is refreshed and queried, then preventive_count=3, reactive_count=2, cancelled-state included per §9.16 WHERE clause.
3. Given the unique index on (org_id, site_id, equipment_id, month), when REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY runs, then it succeeds.
4. Given pg_cron extension is present, when migration runs, then `cron.job` table contains `refresh_maintenance_kpis` at `30 2 * * *`; when absent, migration logs a NOTICE and proceeds.

## Test strategy
- RED: maintenance-kpis-mv.test.ts asserting four ACs.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-kpis-mv`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not paste the §9.16 SQL with the literal `<scheduled_date>` placeholder — resolve to the join expression.
- Do not use SECURITY DEFINER without explicit org_id guard.
- Do not refresh non-CONCURRENTLY in cron (would lock readers).
- Do not omit the UNIQUE index — CONCURRENTLY requires it.
- Do not couple to Reporting MV refresh worker — this is a maintenance-owned cron job.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create maintenance_kpis MV with corrected pm_on_time logic + UNIQUE index for CONCURRENTLY + pg_cron daily 02:30 refresh.
pipeline_inputs.details: MV aggregates monthly per-equipment counters. PRD §9.16 has a `<scheduled_date>` placeholder — resolve to subquery against maintenance_schedules. SECURITY INVOKER preserves RLS for downstream MNT-006/MNT-031 dashboards. Downtime events feeding OEE MTBF/MTTR are published via foundation T-112 outbox dispatcher — this MV is a downstream consumer of those events.
acceptance_criteria: Given migration runs, when maintenance_kpis is queried, then it returns rows grouped by org_id+site_id+equipment_id+month with correct counters per §9.16
Given 6 MWOs in orgA, when MV refreshed, then counters match expected (preventive=3, reactive=2)
Given the UNIQUE index, when REFRESH MV CONCURRENTLY runs, then it succeeds
Given pg_cron extension is present, when migration runs, then cron.job has refresh_maintenance_kpis at 30 2 * * *; when absent, migration logs NOTICE and proceeds
test_strategy: Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test maintenance-kpis-mv`
risk_red_lines: Do not leave `<scheduled_date>` placeholder in SQL — resolve to join
Do not SECURITY DEFINER without org_id guard
Do not REFRESH without CONCURRENTLY in cron
